content: The 2024–2025 SOTA push for scalable active learning agent memory can be reframed as a evolutionary developmental process: just as a biological agent (any living organism) curates its epigenetic memory to only prioritize experiences that improve future adaptive decision-making, cutting-edge active learning agents can leverage this 'epigenetic gating' principle to prune redundant stored experiences instead of scaling raw memory capacity. This aligns curiosity-driven exploration (a core focus of modern active agent research) with the biological drive for agents to seek out novel environmental stimuli that update adaptive memory, rather than storing every encountered datapoint.
